evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my Capacitor app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show a circle highlight on each tracked barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes a complete BarcodeAr integration into EmptyApp.js including plugin init, context, camera setup, BarcodeArSettings, BarcodeAr mode, BarcodeArView connected to a DOM element, and a highlightProvider returning BarcodeArCircleHighlight. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core, scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode, and npx cap sync)
Setup checklist mentions iOS Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription
await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present and awaited before any other Scandit call
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (current API used)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
BarcodeAr.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource(camera) is called
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called
new BarcodeArSettings() is present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
Only Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Symbology.Code128 are enabled in the BarcodeArSettings (semantic)
new BarcodeAr(settings) is present (context NOT passed to BarcodeAr constructor)
new BarcodeArView({ is present
BarcodeArView is constructed with context and barcodeAr in its options object (semantic)
connectToElement( is called and awaited on barcodeArView
barcodeArView.highlightProvider is assigned an object with highlightForBarcode
new BarcodeArCircleHighlight(barcode, BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset is returned from highlightForBarcode
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan AR to ProductListApp.js. When a barcode is newly tracked, add it to the scannedProducts array and re-render the product list. Show a rectangle highlight on each tracked barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeAr to ProductListApp.js alongside the existing DOM rendering code. A BarcodeArListener is added to push newly tracked barcodes to scannedProducts and call renderProductList. All existing DOM hooks, state, and render functions are preserved.

assertions.text: await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
BarcodeAr.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
context.setFrameSource( is called
new BarcodeArSettings() is present
new BarcodeAr(settings) is present (context NOT passed to BarcodeAr constructor)
barcodeAr.addListener( is called with a didUpdateSession callback
session.addedTrackedBarcodes is accessed to get new barcodes
scannedProducts.push( or equivalent is called with barcode data from session
renderProductList() is called after pushing new barcodes
new BarcodeArView({ is present
BarcodeArView is constructed with context and barcodeAr in its options object (semantic)
connectToElement( is called on barcodeArView
barcodeArView.highlightProvider is assigned with a highlightForBarcode returning BarcodeArRectangleHighlight
Existing scannedProducts variable is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)
Existing renderProductList function is preserved
Existing updateTotal / totalLabel logic is preserved
Existing clearButton / onClearTapped logic is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to show an info annotation on each tracked barcode in my Capacitor MatrixScan AR app. The annotation should show the barcode data as a body row and a header that says 'Scanned'. Use Medium width.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeArView.annotationProvider with annotationForBarcode returning a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation with a header (text='Scanned'), a body with one BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent containing the barcode data, and width set to Medium.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned an object
annotationForBarcode is a method on the annotation provider
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed inside annotationForBarcode
annotation.width is set to BarcodeArInfoAnnotationWidthPreset.Medium
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader() is constructed and assigned to annotation.header
header.text is set to 'Scanned' or equivalent
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent() is constructed
component.text is set to barcode.data or equivalent
annotation.body is set to an array containing the body component
The annotationForBarcode method returns the annotation (async / Promise)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, highlightProvider) is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to show a popover annotation with two buttons — 'Accept' and 'Reject' — when a user taps a barcode highlight. When 'Accept' is tapped, log 'accepted' with the barcode data. When 'Reject' is tapped, log 'rejected'.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeArView.annotationProvider with annotationForBarcode returning a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation with two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances and a listener with didTapButton checking buttonIndex to log accept or reject.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned
new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, [acceptButton, rejectButton]) or equivalent is constructed
annotation.annotationTrigger = BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap is set
Two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances are created
annotation.listener is assigned with a didTapButton callback
buttonIndex is used in the didTapButton callback to distinguish accept from reject
console.log or equivalent is called with 'accepted' / 'rejected' and barcode data
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, highlightProvider) is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to show a status icon on each tracked barcode. Barcodes where the data length is greater than 8 should show a warning icon (yellow exclamation) with the text 'Long code'. Others should show a checkmark icon (green) with the text 'OK'.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeArView.annotationProvider with annotationForBarcode returning a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation with icon and text set based on barcode.data.length > 8 condition.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned
new BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is constructed inside annotationForBarcode
annotation.text is set to 'Long code' or equivalent when data length > 8
annotation.text is set to 'OK' or equivalent otherwise
annotation.icon is set using ScanditIconBuilder or a ScanditIcon for both branches
A yellow/warning color (e.g. #FBC02C or similar) is used for the long code icon
A green/checkmark color or icon is used for the OK case
barcode.data.length > 8 is present
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, highlightProvider) is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Capacitor MatrixScan AR app, change the highlight to a circle highlight that has a checkmark icon inside it, and give the circle a solid green brush. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill updates barcodeArView.highlightProvider to return a BarcodeArCircleHighlight built with the Icon preset, sets a green Brush, and assigns a ScanditIcon built with ScanditIconBuilder using the Checkmark icon type to highlight.icon.

assertions.text: window.barcodeArView.highlightProvider is assigned an object with highlightForBarcode
new BarcodeArCircleHighlight(barcode, BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset.Icon) is constructed inside highlightForBarcode
highlight.brush is assigned a new Brush with a green color
highlight.icon is assigned a ScanditIcon
new ScanditIconBuilder() is used to build the icon
.withIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark) is called on the ScanditIconBuilder
.build() is called on the ScanditIconBuilder
ScanditIconBuilder is imported from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core'
BarcodeArCircleHighlight is imported from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode'
BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset is imported from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode'
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera lifecycle) is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Capacitor MatrixScan AR app, show the torch button, the zoom control, and the camera switch button in the BarcodeArView, and default to the front (user-facing) camera. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeArView.shouldShowTorchControl, shouldShowZoomControl, and shouldShowCameraSwitchControl to true, and sets viewSettings.defaultCameraPosition to CameraPosition.UserFacing before the view is created.

assertions.text: window.barcodeArView.shouldShowTorchControl = true is set
window.barcodeArView.shouldShowZoomControl = true is set
window.barcodeArView.shouldShowCameraSwitchControl = true is set
viewSettings.defaultCameraPosition is set to CameraPosition.UserFacing
CameraPosition is imported from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core'
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, highlightProvider) is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to render my own fully custom highlight view (a React-style custom component) for each tracked barcode in my Capacitor MatrixScan AR app instead of the built-in rectangle. How do I do that? File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeArCustomHighlight is not available on Capacitor (only React Native and Flutter), and instead customizes the built-in highlights via the brush and icon properties of BarcodeArRectangleHighlight or BarcodeArCircleHighlight.

assertions.text: The answer states that BarcodeArCustomHighlight is not available on Capacitor (semantic)
The deprecated/unavailable BarcodeArCustomHighlight type is NOT used in the produced code (semantic)
The answer directs the user to customize the built-in highlight via the brush property (semantic)
The answer directs the user to customize the built-in highlight via the icon property (semantic)
BarcodeArRectangleHighlight or BarcodeArCircleHighlight is used as the highlight type (semantic)
